title: verbascum thapsiforme subsp. cuspidatum
intro: Verbascum thapsiforme subsp. cuspidatum, commonly known as mullein, is a biennial plant that belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues such as respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds. The health benefits of mullein include reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and soothing coughs, while also exhibiting antiviral and antifungal properties. Mullein can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Polish journal of pharmacology and pharmacy", Verbascum thapsiforme subsp. cuspidatum benefits include its antiviral activity, which shows a significant decrease in the virus titer by about 2.5 log at non-toxic concentrations.
